items.name: Jamesob's guide to running SOTA LLMs locally
items.source_story_title: Jamesob's guide to running SOTA LLMs locally
items.summary: Practical guide to running strong local LLMs on your own hardware.
items.why_included: The thread produced unusually concrete, hardware-grounded discussion of local LLM setups.
items.evidence: A commenter called it 'a great guide' and the thread turned into a detailed back-and-forth on GPUs, quantization, and local throughput.

items.name: pxpipe
items.source_story_title: 60% Fable cost cut by converting code to images and having the model OCR it
items.summary: Image-token pipeline that cuts Fable costs by OCR'ing code as images.
items.why_included: A concrete cost-cutting trick with visible technical discussion around image-token tradeoffs.
items.evidence: Commenters called the approach 'really fascinating' and debated whether image tokens can cut cost without wrecking quality.

items.name: Mcpsnoop
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Mcpsnoop – Wireshark for MCP (transparent proxy and live TUI)
items.summary: Transparent proxy and live TUI for MCP traffic.
items.why_included: Commenters strongly liked the auditability angle and said it fills a real MCP visibility gap.
items.evidence: Readers said it gives '100% visibility into the back and forth' and called it 'awesome'.

items.name: WireMCP
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Mcpsnoop – Wireshark for MCP (transparent proxy and live TUI)
items.summary: Wireshark MCP bridge for inspecting MCP traffic.
items.why_included: A useful adjacent tool surfaced directly from the Mcpsnoop discussion.
items.evidence: A commenter pointed to WireMCP as an existing Wireshark MCP, and another replied, 'Bingo! Great find.'

items.name: Wordgard
items.source_story_title: Wordgard: In-browser rich-text editor from the creator of ProseMirror
items.summary: In-browser rich-text editor from ProseMirror's creator.
items.why_included: A fresh editor with a clear technical thesis drew strong curiosity and praise.
items.evidence: People called it 'staggeringly brilliant' and 'beautiful', then asked whether it's really worth leaving ProseMirror.

items.name: Safari MCP server
items.source_story_title: The Safari MCP server for web developers
items.summary: Apple/WebKit MCP server for browser automation and web debugging.
items.why_included: The thread mapped a real browser-testing gap and surfaced several strong adjacent tools.
items.evidence: Commenters said Safari's MCP could fill a real browser-testing gap, then recommended Playwright-CLI and Chrome DevTools MCP as adjacent options.

items.name: Playwright-CLI
items.source_story_title: The Safari MCP server for web developers
items.summary: CLI-first browser automation for coding agents.
items.why_included: A commenter recommended it as a faster, more token-efficient browser workflow.
items.evidence: A commenter said it 'works much faster for me than the MCP servers I tried'.

items.name: Chrome DevTools MCP
items.source_story_title: The Safari MCP server for web developers
items.summary: Chrome's official MCP devtools server for deep profiling.
items.why_included: People reported concrete debugging wins with it on real pages.
items.evidence: A commenter said they've used it since Nov 2025 and profiled a slow React page until it hit sub-100ms responsiveness.

items.name: tlaplus-process-studio
items.source_story_title: Hunting a 16-year-old SQLite WAL bug with TLA+
items.summary: Local TLA+ modeling app for high-level state-machine work.
items.why_included: A niche but concrete modeling tool with its own canonical home.
items.evidence: The author said 'I made' a local modeling tool and linked both the site and GitHub repo.

items.name: Bramble
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Bramble – Local-first password manager
items.summary: Local-first, P2P password manager with an encrypted vault.
items.why_included: The local-first privacy angle got immediate traction from commenters.
items.evidence: Readers said 'This looks like something I've been looking for!' and praised the local-first, P2P design.
